story_clusters.summary: India and South Korea have agreed to double bilateral trade and launch a ‘digital bridge’ to enhance economic ties, as announced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Lee Unveil. The two countries also decided to start negotiations to upgrade their existing economic pact and explore a new free trade agreement. Additionally, they expanded cooperation in shipbuilding, targeting a ₹2.2 lakh crore maritime initiative.

articles.title: Power to review judgement or order under Section 114 and Order 47 CPC can’t be exercised suo moto; APTE set asides CERC’s modification order
articles.summary: The Appellate Tribunal (APTE) set aside an order issued by the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) after finding that the Commission had violated natural justice by issuing a direction without hearing the appellant. The tribunal ruled that the power to review judgments or orders under Section 114 and Order 47 of the Civil Procedure Code (CPC) cannot be exercised suo moto. This decision underscores the importance of procedural fairness in regulatory actions.
articles.raw_summary: We find it ironical that the Commission has ignored this basic tenet of natural justice by issuing the direction in question without hearing the appellant. Thus, on this count also, the impugned order cannot be sustained.  The post Power to review judgement or order under Section 114 and Order 47 CPC can’t be exercised suo moto; APTE set asides CERC’s modification order appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Chhattisgarh HC Stayed GST Recovery Subject to Pre-Deposit Compliance
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court granted a stay on GST recovery for a taxpayer but made it conditional on compliance with pre-deposit requirements and other procedural conditions outlined under Section 112 and CBIC circulars. The court emphasized that adherence to these conditions is mandatory for securing relief. This decision underscores the importance of procedural compliance in tax-related legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Court permitted the taxpayer to obtain a stay by following the procedure under Section 112 and relevant CBIC circulars. It emphasized that compliance with pre-deposit and undertaking conditions is mandatory. The decision highlights the role of procedural compliance in securing relief. The post Chhattisgarh HC Stayed GST Recovery Subject to Pre-Deposit Compliance appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: Section 23(4) of India's Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, was added in 2019 to clarify the completion of statements of claim and defence in arbitration proceedings. This discrepancy has created confusion among arbitrators and legal practitioners about procedural requirements, potentially impacting arbitration efficiency and fairness in India.

The marginal note employs the plural noun “ Statements ” unlike sub-section 4 which uses the singular noun “ The statement ”. articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that legal representatives challenging an arbitral award must file an application under Section 34 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, and cannot use Article 227 of the Constitution or Section 115 of the Code of Civil Procedure. The Court held that Section 34 is the exclusive remedy for such challenges. This decision clarifies the legal recourse available to aggrieved parties in arbitration disputes.

articles.title: ITC Cannot Be Denied for Supplier Default: Key GST Rulings Explained
articles.summary: Indian courts have ruled that bona fide buyers cannot be denied Input Tax Credit (ITC) due to their supplier's default in GST payments, unless fraud is involved. The rulings clarify that tax liability cannot be shifted to the recipient without evidence of wrongdoing. This decision provides certainty for businesses on GST compliance and ITC eligibility.
articles.raw_summary: Courts held that bona fide buyers cannot lose ITC due to supplier non-payment. The key takeaway is that liability cannot shift to the recipient without fraud. The post ITC Cannot Be Denied for Supplier Default: Key GST Rulings Explained appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: No Retrospective Laundering: Tribunal Quashes Bulk PMLA Attachments, Retains Only Rs 25 Cr as Proceeds of Crime
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that transactions occurring before an alleged crime cannot be treated as proceeds of crime under the PMLA unless a clear link is established. It set aside most attachments due to lack of foundational evidence, retaining only Rs 25 crore as proceeds of crime. The decision underscores the need for direct evidence linking transactions to criminal activity. This ruling impacts how PMLA cases are handled in the future.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that transactions predating the alleged crime cannot be treated as proceeds of crime without a clear link. It set aside most attachments due to absence of foundational evidence. The decision reinforces the necessity of establishing a direct nexus under PMLA. articles.title: Reverse Burden Applies: Tribunal Upholds Bank Freeze Where Source of Funds Not Proved under PMLA
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), the burden of proof shifts to the accused to demonstrate the legitimacy of their funds once money laundering allegations are established. In this case, the accused failed to prove the source of their funds, leading the tribunal to uphold the continuation of a bank account freeze and seizure of assets. The ruling reinforces the strict enforcement mechanisms of the PMLA, which is a key anti-money laundering law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that once allegations of money laundering are established, the burden shifts to the accused. Failure to prove legitimate income led to continuation of freezing and seizure. articles.title: Equivalent Value Doctrine Affirmed: Tribunal Upholds Attachment Even for Pre-Offence Properties under PMLA
articles.summary: A tribunal has upheld the attachment of properties under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), even if they are not directly linked to a crime, as long as their value is equivalent to the proceeds of crime. This ruling affirms the 'equivalent value doctrine,' allowing authorities to seize assets when the original crime proceeds are untraceable. The decision impacts how enforcement agencies handle asset attachment in money laundering cases.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether properties unconnected to crime could be attached under PMLA. The Tribunal held that equivalent value assets can be attached when proceeds of crime are untraceable. The post Equivalent Value Doctrine Affirmed: Tribunal Upholds Attachment Even for Pre-Offence Properties under PMLA appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bombay Quashed Section 148 Notice Due to Issuance by Wrong Authority
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court invalidated a tax reassessment notice under Section 148 because it was issued by a jurisdictional officer instead of a faceless officer as required. The ruling highlights strict compliance requirements for tax authorities under India's faceless assessment regime. This decision impacts future tax notices and reassessment procedures nationwide.

articles.title: Govt may hike FDI limit in pension sector; Bill likely in Monsoon Session
articles.summary: The Indian government is considering increasing the foreign direct investment (FDI) limit in the pension sector to 100%, potentially through a bill in the upcoming Monsoon Session of Parliament. This move aligns with recent liberalization in the insurance sector. Additionally, proposals include separating the NPS Trust from the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) to ensure independent management.
articles.raw_summary: The government is poised to significantly boost foreign direct investment in the pension sector, potentially raising the limit to 100%. A bill to this effect is anticipated in upcoming parliamentary sessions. This move mirrors the insurance sector's recent FDI liberalization. Additionally, proposals include separating the NPS Trust from the PFRDA, aiming for independent management.

articles.title: SC Recognises Commuter Safety as Integral Facet of Article 21; Issues Nationwide Interim Directions for Prevention of Highway Accidents
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that commuter safety is a fundamental aspect of the Right to Life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The Court issued nationwide interim directions to prevent highway accidents, emphasizing the state's duty to ensure a safe environment. The ruling underscores the positive mandate on the state to preserve and value human life. The directions apply across India and have immediate legal and policy implications for road safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: The Right to Life enshrined under Article 21 of the Constitution is not merely a guarantee against the unlawful taking of life, but a positive mandate upon the State to ensure a safe environment where human life is preserved and valued. articles.title: Service Tax Liability Confirmed on Sub-Contractor Due to Independent Taxability of Services
articles.summary: The CESTAT Allahabad upheld a demand of ₹1,95,356 towards Service Tax for the financial year 2015–16, along with interest and penalties, against Manoj Kumar Gupta, who was registered for providing 'Business Auxiliary Services.' The energy markets have experienced significant volatility since the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran on 28 February. The situation could impact global oil supplies and prices, which are critical for India's energy needs and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

articles.title: Oil prices spike again following latest standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices surged after a standoff between the U.S. and Iran disrupted tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil shipping route. U.S. crude oil prices rose 6.4% to $87.88 per barrel, while Brent crude climbed 6.5% to $96.25 per barrel. The conflict raises concerns for India, a major oil importer, due to potential supply disruptions and higher fuel costs.
articles.raw_summary: The ongoing conflict with the U.S. and Iran prevented tankers from passing through. The price of U.S. crude oil increased 6.4% to $87.88 per barrel after trading resumed on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, climbed 6.5% to $96.25 per barrel.

articles.title: Equivalent Value Attachment Valid: Tribunal Upholds PMLA Action in DJB ₹38 Cr Scam Case
articles.summary: A tribunal upheld the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) action in a ₹38 crore Delhi Jal Board (DJB) scam case. The tribunal ruled that attaching property of equivalent value is valid when proceeds of crime are untraceable. This decision reinforces the government's ability to recover ill-gotten gains under PMLA, impacting financial crime enforcement in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether property not directly linked to crime could be attached. The Tribunal held that attachment of equivalent value is valid when proceeds of crime are untraceable. This ruling highlights the interplay between state and central judicial jurisdictions in bail matters.
articles.raw_summary: The bench made clear that in case Khera applies for anticipatory bail before the court having jurisdiction in Assam, the order passed by the apex court would not have any adverse inference while deciding such a plea.

articles.title: Sabarimala reference in SC: Managing board wants temple’s rights to prevail over individual’s rights
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is examining a reference related to the Sabarimala temple dispute, where the managing board's lawyer argued that the temple's rights should take precedence over individual rights. The lawyer also acknowledged the state's potential authority to open the temple to all Hindus, but only to ensure entry for all Hindu classes and sections. The case involves balancing religious customs with constitutional rights.
articles.raw_summary: However, board's lawyer did not rule out state's right to throw open public temple to all classes and sections of Hindus. Such a right would be limited only to ensuring all Hindus gain entry, he said.

This decision clarifies the legal framework for tax authorities assessing transactions between related entities.

articles.title: FY27 capex growth of states pegged at 8–10%: report
articles.summary: State governments in India are expected to slow their capital spending growth to 8–10% in FY27, down from a strong FY26, due to rising revenue expenses and slower revenue growth. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia could further strain state finances. Fiscal discipline will be critical as states balance welfare spending with infrastructure investments, with top states continuing to prioritize development projects.
articles.raw_summary: State capital spending growth is set to slow in FY27. This moderation follows a strong FY26. Rising revenue expenses and slower revenue growth are key factors. Geopolitical issues in West Asia could further impact state finances. Fiscal discipline will be crucial for states balancing welfare and investment needs. Top states continue to prioritize infrastructure development.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Additions as Bogus Purchases Cannot Be Based on Suspicion Without Evidence
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that tax authorities cannot disallow purchases solely based on suspicion without concrete evidence. The case involved a taxpayer whose purchases were supported by documents and sales were accepted, but the tax department had estimated additions without rejecting the books or providing independent evidence. The ITAT deleted these additions, setting a precedent for future tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether purchases can be disallowed when supported by documents and sales are accepted. ITAT held that estimation without rejecting books or independent evidence is unsustainable. The post ITAT Deletes Additions as Bogus Purchases Cannot Be Based on Suspicion Without Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru .

Reforms also target faster approvals for duty-free imports under the Advance Authorisation Scheme.

articles.title: Is the Indian middle class dream over? Saurabh Mukherjea on the 3 forces crushing your future
articles.summary: India's middle class is in deep trouble. Education no longer guarantees prosperity. Jobs are disappearing, especially in IT. Wages are not keeping pace with rising costs. Household debt is soaring, often for consumption. AI is further threatening jobs. This consumption engine slowdown impacts the economy. The middle class has limited political voice.

articles.title: El Niño and geopolitical tensions cloud India’s rural economy: A somber 2026 outlook
articles.summary: India’s rural economy faces pressure in 2026 from a likely El Niño-driven weak monsoon and rising input costs due to US-Iran tensions. Lower farm output, weak incomes, and elevated inflation may hit consumption, while higher fertiliser subsidies could strain fiscal balances, keeping the overall outlook cautious.

articles.title: From protests to paychecks: How state unrest triggered a wage rethink
articles.summary: The Indian government is setting a national floor for minimum wages, which will require states to adjust their own minimum wages accordingly. Once implemented, the monthly wage will be calculated as 26 times the final daily wage. This move aims to standardize wage structures across states and address wage disparities triggered by unrest. The policy will impact millions of workers and compel state governments to revise their wage policies.
articles.raw_summary: Once a national floor is set, states will be compelled to revise their minimum wages accordingly, as wages cannot be set below the national floor. The monthly payout will be 26 times the final daily wage.

The Employees' State Insurance Corporation's wage ceiling is also being reviewed.

This move aims to prevent workers from being excluded from social security due to rising wages. Discussions are underway with stakeholders to finalize the new ceiling. The Employees' State Insurance Corporation ceiling is also being reviewed.

Discussions focused on shipbuilding, AI, semiconductors, and critical technologies, aiming to strengthen their strategic partnership amidst global economic instability. Both leaders also addressed regional peace and bolstering their economic cooperation.

Both leaders also addressed regional peace and bolstering their economic cooperation.

articles.title: I-PAC emails staff saying West Bengal ops ‘paused’, days ahead of polls. TMC says ‘all is well’
articles.summary: I-PAC, a political consultancy firm, reportedly paused its operations in West Bengal ahead of upcoming elections. The Trinamool Congress (TMC) stated that all I-PAC employees would be taken care of, though it did not officially confirm the shutdown. The situation highlights potential disruptions in political campaigning infrastructure during the election period.
articles.raw_summary: Though Trinamool did not officially confirm shutdown of the agency, it reiterated, in a press conference Monday, that all I-PAC employees will be taken care of.

articles.title: India's first maritime-focused lender eyes $1 bln FY27 fundraise
articles.summary: Sagarmala Finance Corp. will raise 100 billion rupees in FY27. This funding will support ports, shipbuilding, and waterways. The company will borrow through bonds, term loans, and foreign currency. Sagarmala aims to disburse up to 90 billion rupees in loans by March 2027. It also seeks government equity infusion to support its growth.

It also seeks government equity infusion to support its growth.

articles.title: Basmati exporters of Punjab urge Goyal to restructure & initiate reforms in development body BEDF
articles.summary: Exporters from Punjab have urged Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to restructure and reform the Basmati Export Development Foundation (BEDF), citing a recent 100% hike in mandatory contract registration fees. The BEDF, a government-backed body, has faced criticism over its fee structure, which exporters argue is burdensome. The demand reflects concerns over trade facilitation and regulatory costs impacting India's basmati rice export sector.
articles.raw_summary: The BEDF has been under limelight ever since it hiked the mandatory contract registration fee by more than 100 per cent last year

This shift could impact Kerala's political dynamics and future policy directions.

This initiative could significantly enhance care for millions.

articles.title: Confession Evidence In Organized Crime Legislations: A Critical Legal Perspective
articles.summary: The article examines how India's organized crime laws, such as those under the UAPA and NDPS Act, relax traditional evidence rules to secure convictions. Courts must balance these provisions with safeguards to prevent misuse and ensure that evidentiary flexibility does not violate fundamental rights. The discussion underscores the tension between effective law enforcement and the protection of individual liberties.
articles.raw_summary: Organized crime laws relax traditional evidence rules to secure convictions. Courts balance this with safeguards against misuse. Key takeaway: evidentiary flexibility must not violate rights. The post Confession Evidence In Organized Crime Legislations: A Critical Legal Perspective appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GAAR clarity: Legacy investments shielded but substance remains crucial
articles.summary: According to the amendment, cases where GAAR was invoked prior to March 31, 2026, fall outside its scope. However, it remains to be seen how Indian income-tax authorities will deal with such cases, write tax law experts Bijal Ajinkya and Viraj Doshi of Khaitan & Co.

articles.title: India drops proposal to mandate national ID app Aadhaar on smartphones after pushback
articles.summary: India's government has dropped a proposal to pre-install the Aadhaar app on smartphones. This decision follows opposition from smartphone makers like Apple and Samsung. They raised concerns about security, costs, and manufacturing. Privacy advocates also criticized Aadhaar due to past data leaks. The government previously withdrew a similar mandate for a telecom app.

They raised concerns about security, costs, and manufacturing. Privacy advocates also criticized Aadhaar due to past data leaks. The government previously withdrew a similar mandate for a telecom app.

articles.title: Telangana releases caste survey: Backward Classes form 56.33%, Madiga at 10.3% top caste
articles.summary: The Telangana government released a caste survey showing that Backward Classes constitute 56.33% of the population, with the Madiga community at 10.3%. BC Welfare and Transport Minister Ponnam Prabhakar called it a milestone and urged the Centre to adopt a similar nationwide exercise for social justice. The survey highlights caste demographics that could influence future welfare policies and discussions on social justice in India.
articles.raw_summary: BC Welfare and Transport Minister Ponnam Prabhakar termed the survey a milestone and urged the Centre to adopt a similar approach across the country. “The Telangana survey should serve as an inspiration for a nationwide exercise to ensure social justice,” he said.

articles.title: Core sector activity contracts 0.4% in March 2026 on West Asia impact, worst in 19 months
articles.summary: India's core sector activity, which includes key industries like coal, crude oil, and electricity, contracted by 0.4% in March 2026 due to disruptions from West Asia. Four out of eight core sectors shrank, marking the worst performance in 19 months. The full fiscal year 2025-26 saw the lowest growth since the COVID-19 pandemic. The government's data highlights potential economic challenges that could prompt policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Data released by the government shows that four out of the eight core sectors of the economy contracted in March 2026; growth in the full year of 2025-26 was the lowest since the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: Slight increase in unemployment rate in March
articles.summary: The overall Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) for persons aged 15 and above declined to 55.4% in March from 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR also fell to 58% from 58.7% in the same period. These figures highlight employment trends that could influence government policy decisions or welfare schemes aimed at addressing job market challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Overall Labour Force Participation Rate among persons aged 15 and above was 55.4% in March, lower than 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR was 58% in March compared to 58.7% in previous month.

articles.title: Wholesale price inflation rises 3.88% in March on surge in crude rates
articles.summary: Wholesale Price Index (WPI)-based inflation in India rose to 3.88% in March, up from 2.13% in February and 2.25% in March last year. The increase is attributed to a surge in crude oil prices. Rising inflation may prompt the government or the Reserve Bank of India to consider policy measures to stabilize prices and manage economic impact.

articles.title: On mission to revive Capt Vijayakanth’s legacy, why Prabhakaran thinks DMK tie-up is DMDK’s best bet
articles.summary: DMDK founder Captain Vijayakanth passed away in December 2023, and his son V Prabhakaran has taken over as the party's leader. Prabhakaran has allied with the DMK for the upcoming Tamil Nadu assembly elections, aiming to revive his father's political legacy. The alliance reflects strategic political maneuvering ahead of state polls.

articles.title: From rebel to star campaigner: How Shashi Tharoor found his way back in Congress
articles.summary: Shashi Tharoor, a senior Congress leader, has realigned with the party leadership after months of public divergence. He has since been actively criticizing the central government in Parliament and campaigning for the United Democratic Front (UDF) in Kerala. This shift highlights internal party dynamics and opposition strategies ahead of elections.
articles.raw_summary: After months of mixed signals, public divergence   praising opponents, Tharoor has realigned with Congress leadership—attacking Centre in Parliament   campaigning for UDF in Kerala.

articles.title: TCS Nashik case—VHP urges women safety in corporate world, caution in hiring from ‘specific religious group’
articles.summary: The Vishwa Hindu Parishad (VHP), an affiliate of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), has written to top industry bodies expressing concern over the TCS Nashik case, which has raised questions about women's safety in corporate workplaces. The VHP urged caution in hiring from 'specific religious groups' to maintain social harmony, stating that the case has shaken public trust in the corporate sector. The letter highlights the need for measures to ensure workplace safety and social cohesion.
articles.raw_summary: RSS affiliate writes to top industry bodies, saying TCS case has 'shaken people's social trust in corporate space' on women’s safety at workplaces   maintaining nation’s social harmony.

articles.summary: Oil prices surged over 5% after fears grew that the US-Iran ceasefire might collapse, reigniting concerns about disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This strait is a critical global oil shipping route, and any instability could impact India's energy imports and fuel prices. The situation highlights vulnerabilities in global trade and energy markets that may prompt policy discussions in India.

articles.title: Dismissed over misconduct claim, IAF pilot fought long legal battle. 33 yrs on, SC restores his honour
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an Indian Air Force officer's dismissal in 1993 over misconduct charges was illegal. After a 33-year legal battle, the court directed the Air Force to formally retire the officer 'in the normal manner.' The ruling highlights issues of due process and employment rights in the military. The officer's honour has been restored, setting a precedent for similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: Top court declares officer's 1993 termination illegal, directing the Air Force to formally sign him off 'in the normal manner' as if he had retired from service.

articles.title: Saradha scam trials: 13 yrs on, Bengal’s biggest financial fraud stuck in a ‘stalemate’ in courts
articles.summary: Thirteen years after India's biggest financial fraud—the Saradha scam—court proceedings remain stalled in West Bengal. Key issues include pending charge sheets, missing witnesses, absent accused, and crucial documents not being filed. ThePrint's analysis of court records reveals systemic delays in the judicial process. The case, involving massive financial losses for thousands of investors, reflects broader challenges in India's legal and regulatory frameworks for financial crimes.
articles.raw_summary: ThePrint’s analysis of court records shows a pattern of stalled proceedings: charge sheets pending, witnesses not deposing, accused not being produced and crucial documents missing.

This interaction highlights ongoing defense cooperation between India and the US, which could influence future defense procurement or strategic policies in India.

articles.title: Trump's Naval Blockade of Hormuz: Kinetic Economic Coercion
articles.summary: The article examines a proposed U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz under former President Trump, a move that could disrupt global oil shipments. Given India's heavy reliance on oil imports from the Middle East, such a blockade could threaten energy security and impact fuel prices. The analysis highlights potential economic and geopolitical consequences for India, including trade disruptions and strategic responses.

articles.title: India’s current account deficit may rise to 2% of GDP in FY27 if oil stays at $82–87: CRISIL
articles.summary: Rising oil prices could push India's current account deficit to a concerning two percent of GDP, as highlighted by a Crisil report. This prediction hinges on the volatile global market conditions we’re currently facing. While US tariff reductions may bolster exports to some extent, the ongoing unrest in West Asia is another pivotal aspect that could affect the situation.

articles.title: Fear runs deep as Noida labourers return to a heavily policed factory floor
articles.summary: Thousands of labourers in Noida held a two-day protest, which ended abruptly after police presence and threats of detention silenced the workers. The incident raises concerns about worker rights and the use of police force in industrial disputes. Labourers returned to work under heavy policing, reflecting a chilling effect on protests.
articles.raw_summary: The threat of detention and arrests appears to have had a chilling effect on labourers, who have now fallen silent, bringing an end to a two-day protest that drew thousands.

articles.title: US security agency is using Anthropic's Mythos despite blacklist: Axios
articles.summary: The US National Security Agency (NSA) is reportedly using Anthropic's advanced AI tool, Mythos Preview, despite the Pentagon flagging supply-chain risks associated with the company. The AI model is noted for its strong capabilities in coding and autonomous tasks, which experts warn could enhance cyberattack capabilities. Discussions between the US administration and Anthropic have also taken place regarding its use.
articles.raw_summary: The US National Security Agency is reportedly using Anthropic's advanced AI tool, Mythos Preview. This comes even after the Pentagon flagged the company for supply-chain risks. The AI model is said to be highly capable in coding and autonomous tasks. Experts suggest its abilities could significantly enhance cyberattack capabilities. Discussions between the US administration and Anthropic have also taken place.

articles.title: SC refuses to entertain plea seeking guidelines for display of Ashoka Chakra
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea seeking guidelines for the display of the Ashoka Chakra, a national symbol. The petitioner had highlighted an installation in Varanasi, but the bench did not take up the matter. This decision may influence future discussions on the display and regulation of national symbols in public spaces.
articles.raw_summary: The petitioner showed a picture of the Ashoka Chakra installed at a roundabout in Varanasi, but the bench refused to entertain the plea.

articles.title: The need for due diligence in urban road planning
articles.summary: The article, authored by Ramanath Jha from ORF and published in HT Insight Economic Policy, highlights the need for thorough due diligence in urban road planning. It emphasizes that poorly planned roads can lead to long-term inefficiencies and safety risks, underscoring the importance of evidence-based decision-making in infrastructure projects. The piece calls for greater scrutiny in urban planning to avoid costly mistakes and ensure sustainable development.
articles.raw_summary: This paper is authored by Ramanath Jha, ORF.

articles.title: Unemployment among individuals aged 15 and above rises marginally to 5.1% in March
articles.summary: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), conducted by the National Statistics Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, shows that unemployment among individuals aged 15 and above rose marginally to 5.1% in March. This data provides insights into employment conditions in India. The findings could inform government policies or welfare schemes aimed at addressing unemployment.
articles.raw_summary: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), conducted by the National Statistics Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), is the primary source of data on activity participation, employment and unemployment conditions of the population.

articles.title: Ajit Pawar plane crash: Maharashtra CID to incorporate zero FIR lodged by NCP (SP) MLA in probe
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Criminal Investigation Department (CID) will include a Zero FIR filed by NCP (SP) MLA Rohit Pawar in Bengaluru as part of its investigation into the January 28 Baramati plane crash that killed Deputy Chief Minister Ajit Pawar. The Zero FIR, typically used to register complaints without jurisdiction constraints, will now be part of the official probe. This move highlights the intersection of legislative oversight and administrative action in addressing the incident.

articles.title: Foreign betting sites pull a prank on taxman
articles.summary: Offshore betting websites created fake pages using a domain similar to the official GST Ahmedabad zone government website (cgst.ahmedabadzone.gov.in). These pages included sub-links redirecting users to actual online gambling platforms, misleading users into believing the sites were legitimate. The tactic appears to be an attempt to evade taxes and regulatory scrutiny. Authorities may need to address this issue to prevent fraud and protect users.
articles.raw_summary: Offshore gaming firms found a way to use the site name cgst.ahmedabadzone.gov.in to create additional, fake pages, complete with sub-links that take the browser to the actual gaming platforms, in claiming legitimacy of these online casinos.

This indicates continued investor confidence in the Indian market.

articles.title: Core sector output fell 0.4% in March, lowest in 19 months
articles.summary: India’s core sector output contracted 0.4% year-on-year in March, marking its lowest level in 19 months, driven by a sharp decline in fertiliser production amid the West Asia conflict and weaker performance across energy-related industries, according to official data released on Monday.

articles.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is in New Delhi for talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Cooperation may also expand into finance, artificial intelligence, and defense. India's role in global production is highlighted.

articles.title: Below-normal monsoon and West Asia conflict cloud India's agriculture outlook: BoB
articles.summary: India's agriculture faces a challenging year. A below-normal monsoon forecast and the West Asian conflict threaten crop production and rural economic growth. The India Meteorological Department predicts rainfall at 92 percent of the Long Period Average, the lowest initial forecast in 25 years. Disruptions in gas supply also affect fertilizer and pesticide availability.

The situation involves Iran, the US, and global oil markets, with potential consequences for India's energy imports and economic stability.

articles.title: Sharp curve, loss of control, 100-ft plunge: What caused Udhampur bus crash that left 20 dead?
articles.summary: A bus traveling from Ramnagar to Udhampur lost control near Kanote village and plunged into a gorge, killing 20 people. The incident raises questions about road safety and transport regulations. The bus was likely operated by a government or state-run entity, highlighting potential governance and infrastructure concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The incident took place near Kanote village when the bus, travelling from Ramnagar to Udhampur, lost control and plunged into a gorge.

articles.title: Rajnath to undertake 3-day visit to Germany; boosting defence, industrial ties main focus
articles.summary: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will undertake a three-day visit to Germany to strengthen defence and industrial cooperation between the two countries. The visit aims to enhance bilateral ties in critical sectors, which could lead to future policy decisions on defence procurement, technology transfer, and trade agreements. The focus on industrial ties suggests potential discussions on manufacturing partnerships and supply chain collaborations.
articles.raw_summary: Rajnath to undertake 3-day visit to Germany; boosting defence, industrial ties main focus

articles.title: TN firecracker unit blast: Bodies charred beyond recognition; kin await DNA tests
articles.summary: A firecracker unit in Tamil Nadu exploded, killing workers whose bodies were charred beyond recognition. The blast occurred while workers were handling raw materials. Relatives await DNA tests to identify the victims. The incident raises concerns about industrial safety regulations and worker welfare.
articles.raw_summary: The blast ripped through the Vanaja Fireworks veranda around 3.30pm as the workers were reportedly handling raw materials and finishing firecrackers

articles.title: Goa: YouTuber booked for derogatory remarks about Catholic saint Francis Xavier
articles.summary: A YouTuber, Gautam Khattar, has been booked in Goa for allegedly making derogatory remarks about Saint Francis Xavier, a Catholic saint. Opposition Congress leader Peter D’Souza is among the complainants, stating the comments were deeply hurtful. The incident has sparked discussions about free speech and religious sensitivities in India.
articles.raw_summary: Opposition Congress leader Peter D’Souza, who is among the complainants against Gautam Khattar, said the comments were deeply hurtful

articles.title: 20 dead, 36 injured as passenger bus rolls into gorge in Jammu and Kashmir’s Udhampur district
articles.summary: A passenger bus traveling from Ramnagar to Udhampur lost control and plunged into a gorge near Kanote village in Jammu and Kashmir’s Udhampur district, resulting in 20 deaths and 36 injuries. The accident raises concerns about road safety and transport infrastructure in the region. Authorities are likely to investigate the cause, which could prompt discussions on policy measures to prevent such incidents.
articles.raw_summary: The accident took place near Kanote village when the bus, travelling from Ramnagar to Udhampur, lost control and plunged into a gorge.

articles.title: Pune: Akola, Wardha touch 45°C as state reels under heat
articles.summary: Akola and Wardha in Maharashtra recorded temperatures of 45°C, making them among the hottest places in the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) confirmed these readings. Extreme heat poses health risks and may prompt government action on climate adaptation or public safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: According to data from the IMD, Akola, Wardha have emerged among the hottest locations in the state; each recording a maximum temperature of 45 degrees Celsius.

articles.title: US admiral explains Trump’s ‘brilliant’ plan that will force Iran to concede defeat
articles.summary: A US admiral described a US strategy to restore free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz and compel Iran to abandon its nuclear ambitions. The plan involves military and diplomatic measures to address regional security concerns. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical oil transit route, and any disruption could impact global energy markets, including India's oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: The US is pursuing to restore free navigation through Strait of Hormuz while compelling Iran to abandon its nuclear ambitions, the admiral said.

articles.title: Pakistan prepares for peace talks that may not happen; confusion clouds Iran, US participation
articles.summary: Pakistan is preparing for potential peace talks with Iran and the US, with US envoys expected to arrive in Islamabad. The talks, scheduled around a ceasefire deadline, remain uncertain. The situation could impact regional stability, which is relevant to India's foreign policy and defence interests.
articles.raw_summary: Donald Trump said his envoys would arrive in Islamabad on Monday evening, just a day before the current ceasefire is set to expire.

articles.title: Not rescue but revolution: Inside Kranti’s radical classroom
articles.summary: Kranti, a small school in the Himalayas, provides radical education to students from red-light districts, aiming to drive social change. The initiative challenges traditional systems by empowering marginalized youth through unconventional teaching methods. While not a government policy itself, the model raises questions about access to education and welfare for vulnerable groups in India.
articles.raw_summary: On the Himalayan slopes, far from the red-light districts where its students come from, a small school carves a new path for social change.

articles.title: Chemicals For Missiles From China On Seized Iran Ship? Nikki Haley's Claim
articles.summary: A ship seized by Iran was reportedly carrying chemicals used for missiles from China, according to a claim by former US Ambassador Nikki Haley. Iran accused the US of 'armed piracy' and refused to participate in further talks in Islamabad. The incident raises concerns about arms proliferation in the region and could impact India's defence and foreign policy considerations.
